name: Beef Jerkey #1
recipeInstructions:
1. Select a 1/2 inch thick flank or top round steak.
2. Trim away all fat, then partially freeze until firm.
3. Slice across the grain into 1/4 to 1/2 inch wide strips.
4. Place meat strips in a shallow dish or heavy plastic bag.
5. Add soy or teriyaki sauce to cover meat. If desired, add onion or garlic powder, or Worcestershire sauce.
6. Toss to coat each piece evenly.
7. Cover and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
8. Remove meat from the marinade and drain excess liquid.
9. Air dry the meat strips for a couple of hours.
10. Arrange the meat strips in a single layer on a fine wire screen or cake cooling rack.
11. Place in a low-temperature oven (175 to 200 degrees) or slowly smoke-cook in a smoker until the meat is completely dried.
12. Store in an air-tight container and serve as a snack or appetizer.
